subject: Computer Networking
title: VLANs and Trunking
description: A 60-second lesson on VLANs and Trunking.

hook.text: One physical switch, three separate networks. VLANs do that without extra hardware.

buildup.text: A VLAN logically groups switch ports. Devices in different VLANs can't see each other at Layer 2.

discovery.text: VLAN tagging adds a small header to each frame identifying its VLAN. The tag is a 12-bit ID.

twist.text: Trunk ports carry multiple VLANs between switches. Without trunks, each VLAN needs its own cable.

climax.text: VLANs boost security: accounting can't sniff engineering's traffic, even on the same switch.

punchline.text: VLANs split one switch into many virtual networks. Smart and cheap.

quiz.question: What is the main benefit of using VLANs?
options: Logically separating networks on the same physical switch
Increasing the speed of all network connections
